title: pi-workflows agent dispatch has no content-identity cache (SHA256 of prompt+opts → cached result); each agent dispatch re-runs even on identical input across invocations + sessions; persistent cache could massively speedup repeated runs (nightly audits with same code)
description: Per R-0014 recon axis 6: Claude Code workflows cache (prompt, opts) → result; longest unchanged prefix returns cached results instantly; survives resume within session. pi-workflows has per-step state persistence (re-runs un-completed steps on resume) but no content-identity cache — identical agent dispatches across invocations or sessions re-run + re-spend tokens. For workflows run repeatedly with same input (nightly audits, idempotent verifications, replay of stable test scenarios), the wasted token cost is significant + the wall-clock latency is unnecessary.

evidence.reference: Part 3 axis 6 — Resume Cache by (Prompt, Opts) Identity — INSPIRE verdict
no cache check before subprocess spawn

impact: Token waste on repeated runs of identical workflows. Cache invalidation puzzles (changed agent behavior → stale cached result) are real risk but mitigatable by explicit cache-clear flag + content-hash including agent.yaml hash + tool-surface hash. Operators running nightly verifications pay for re-computation that yields identical results.
canonical_vocabulary: content-identity caching; SHA256(prompt+opts+agent-spec-hash); persistent agent cache; cache-by-input not cache-by-step-id
proposed_resolution: Optional opt-in cache mode on agent step: 'cache: true | { ttl?: duration, key?: <expression> }'. Compute cache key = SHA256(prompt + JSON.stringify(opts) + agent-spec-content-hash + tool-surface-hash). Store cache in ~/.pi/agent-cache/<hash>.json with metadata (timestamp, agent-spec-version, expires). On dispatch, check cache before spawn; return cached result if hit + not expired. Configuration: 'pi cache clear' / 'pi cache list' / 'pi cache stats' CLI surface. Spec example: steps.audit-file: { agent: file-auditor, cache: true, input: { file: ${{ item }} } }. Risk + mitigation: explicit opt-in only (default off); cache key includes agent-spec hash so spec changes auto-invalidate; ttl bounds staleness; cache-clear command + per-invocation --no-cache flag for forced re-run.
